title: `podman manifest add` unable to set os.version
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

When trying to create or modify a manifest that includes a Windows image, I am unable to set the `os.version` value in the manifest via `podman manifest add ... --os-version`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run the following commands:
   1. `podman manifest create public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1`
   1. `podman manifest add public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1 public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1-windows-amd64 --arch amd64 --os windows`
1. At this point, run `podman manifest inspect public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1` and note that, as expected, there is no `os.version` value under `platform` for the image.
1. Now try to add the image to the manifest with an `os.version`:
   1. `podman manifest add public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1 public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1-windows-amd64 --arch amd64 --os windows --os-version 10.0.17763.1282`
1. Now run `podman manifest inspect public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1` again. Note that there is still no `os.version` value under `platform` for the image. This is incorrect.


**Describe the results you received:**
Output of `podman manifest inspect`:

```
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json",
    "manifests": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
            "size": 1752,
            "digest": "sha256:2a72849bcd7c46518523072f8ad3453c451030a1ddfd2dd9c95bdae3ffdfdc8e",
            "platform": {
                "architecture": "amd64",
                "os": "windows"
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Output of `podman manifest inspect`:

```
{
    "schemaVersion": 2,
    "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json",
    "manifests": [
        {
            "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
            "size": 1752,
            "digest": "sha256:2a72849bcd7c46518523072f8ad3453c451030a1ddfd2dd9c95bdae3ffdfdc8e",
            "platform": {
                "architecture": "amd64",
                "os": "windows",
                "os.version": "10.0.17763.1282"
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I also tried a few other syntax variations, but none of them worked either:

* `--os-version=10.0.17763.1282`
* `--os-version "10.0.17763.1282"`
* `--os-version="10.0.17763.1282"`

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Wed Dec  8 12:41:11 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 15:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running on a MacBook with Big Sur 11.6.2

```
podman (pause) $ podman manifest inspect public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1
{
```

This looks like it worked for me?
```
$ podman -v
podman version 3.4.4
$ rpm -q podman
podman-3.4.4-1.fc35.x86_64
```

I tried it on a linux machine with podman version 3.0.1 and it worked, like you described.

However, when I run these commands on a Mac, it doesn't work. Are there other things I should try to get that environment to work?
Can you get newer versions on the MAC, this could be a remote API problem.
I’m running 3.4.4 on the Mac (both client and server), which is the latest release if I understand correctly.

I just ran podman 3.4.4 against this script, with a reset storage and it works for me on Linux -> Linux.
```
#!/usr/bin/bash -x
/bin/podman --remote manifest create public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1
/bin/podman --remote manifest add public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1 public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1-windows-amd64 --arch amd64 --os windows
/bin/podman --remote manifest inspect public.ecr.aws/u0c8p9d8/nth-test-public:v1.6.1
```


@ashley-cui could you have a look and try to reproduce on mac?

Update from me: I tried using `--remote` and it behaved the same; still unable to set `os.version` on a Mac.

I've gone back to using `docker manifest annotate` commands instead of `podman manifest`, so this is not a pressing issue for me, but it does still seem like a bug.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@flouthoc PTAL. I think this could be a podman-remote issue, could you see if this has been fixed?
Ackd, I can reproduce this bug exists on `podman-remote` side. I'll check this thanks.
@snay2 Thanks, above PR should close this issue and will be present in newer versions of podman.
